## [mas-orchestration]
# MAS Orchestration Rules

## Workflow Model

Each stage runs in its own workspace. mas_gen is responsible for:
1. Writing the MAS document — the single source of truth
2. Outputting [MAS HANDOFF] blocks to tell the user which workspace to switch to next
3. Reviewing results when other workspaces report back

You do NOT switch context internally. You hand off via explicit [MAS HANDOFF] messages.

## Handoff Protocol

When MAS is complete and ready for the next stage, output:

```
[MAS HANDOFF] → rtl_gen
Module  : <ip_name>
MAS     : <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md
Task    : Implement RTL from MAS §2-§8
Input   : <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md
Output  : <ip_name>/rtl/<ip_name>.sv, <ip_name>/list/<ip_name>.f
Switch  : /workspace rtl_gen → /new-ip-rtl
```

## Quality Gates

| Gate | Condition | Next workspace |
|------|-----------|----------------|
| MAS → RTL | All 9 sections complete | `rtl_gen` → `/new-ip-rtl` |
| RTL → TB | lint: 0 errors, 0 warnings | `tb_gen` → `/new-ip-tb` |
| TB → SIM | TB compiles clean | `sim` → `/compile` |
| SIM → LINT | sim: 0 errors, 0 warnings | `lint` → `/lint-all` |

## File Ownership

| Workspace | Writes | Never modifies |
|-----------|--------|----------------|
| `req_gen` | `<ip>/req/<ip>_requirements.md` | everything else |
| `mas_gen` | `<ip>/mas/<ip>_mas.md` | source files |
| `rtl_gen` | `<ip>/rtl/<ip>.sv`, `<ip>/list/<ip>.f` | `tb_*.sv`, `*.md` |
| `tb_gen`  | `<ip>/tb/tb_<ip>.sv`, `<ip>/tb/tc_<ip>.sv` | `<ip>.sv` |
| `sim`     | `<ip>/sim/sim_report.txt` | source files |
| `lint`    | `<ip>/lint/lint_report.txt` | source files |

## Error Recovery

- RTL lint error → user switches to `rtl_gen`, fixes, re-runs `/lint`
- Sim error (DUT bug) → user switches to `rtl_gen`, fixes RTL
- Sim error (TB bug) → user switches to `tb_gen`, fixes TB
- Max sim iterations → output [MAS BLOCKED] report with details

## [rtl-coding-rules]
# RTL Coding Rules (rtl_gen)

## Mandatory Patterns

```systemverilog
// FF block — always nonblocking
always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) q <= '0;
    else        q <= d;
end

// Comb block — always blocking
always_comb begin
    out = '0;          // default assignment (prevents latch)
    case (sel)
        2'b00: out = a;
        2'b01: out = b;
        default: out = '0;
    endcase
end
```

## Forbidden Patterns

```systemverilog
// NEVER: mixed blocking/nonblocking
always_ff @(posedge clk) begin
    a = b;    // WRONG — blocking in FF block
    c <= d;
end

// NEVER: latch (missing else)
always_comb begin
    if (en) out = data;  // WRONG — no else → latch
end

// NEVER: initial in RTL
initial begin  // WRONG — not synthesizable
    reg_val = 0;
end
```

## Width Rules

- Explicit width on all constants: `8'h00`, `1'b0`, `'0` (all zeros)
- Check width match in assignments — no implicit truncation
- Use `$clog2(N)` for address width calculation

## Reset Convention

- Active-low async reset: `always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)`
- Active-high sync reset: `always_ff @(posedge clk)` with `if (rst)`
- Pick ONE per project — document in spec

## ✅ RTL Requirements Extracted from MAS

**Source:** `cortex_m0_core/mas/cortex_m0_core_spec.md` (1785 lines, §1–§9)

### Module Architecture (§2)
- **Name:** `cortex_m0_core` — **flat, monolithic module** (no sub-modules)
- **Parameters:** `IRQ_NUM` (int, default 32, range 1–240), `INIT_PC` (32'h00000004), `INIT_SP` (32'h00000000)
- **Ports (19 total):** HCLK, HRESETn | HADDR[31:0], HBURST[2:0], HMASTLOCK, HPROT[3:0], HSIZE[2:0], HTRANS[1:0], HWDATA[31:0], HWRITE, HRDATA[31:0], HREADY, HRESP | IRQ[IRQ_NUM-1:0], NMI, EVTEXEC | HALTED, DBGRQ, DBGACK

### Pipeline FSM (§3)
- **8 main states:** RESET(3'b000), FETCH(3'b001), DECODE(3'b010), EXECUTE(3'b011), MEM_WAIT(3'b100), STALL(3'b101), SLEEP(3'b110), DEBUG_HALT(3'b111)
- **Exception overlay states:** EXC_ENTRY, EXC_STACKING, EXC_VECTOR_FETCH, EXC_HANDLER, EXC_RETURN
- Full transition table defined with explicit conditions for every state pair
- Stall logic: HREADY=0 freezes pipeline, holds all AHB outputs stable

### ALU (§4.1)
- **19 operations:** ADD, ADC, SUB, SBC, RSB, AND, ORR, EOR, BIC, LSL, LSR, ASR, MUL, MOV, MVN, NOP, CMP, CMN, TST
- **Flags:** N=result[31], Z=(result==0), C/V per arithmetic rules
- **Multiplier:** Single-cycle hardware (default), iterative shift-add fallback

### Instruction Decoder (§4.2)
- **Thumb-16** full decode map + **Thumb-2** (BL, MRS, MSR, DMB, DSB, ISB)
- **13 instruction classes:** ALU, SHIFT, CMP, MOVE, MUL, LOAD, STORE, LOADM, STOREM, BRANCH, BL, BX, SYSTEM
- **14 condition codes** evaluated from APSR flags
- Decoder outputs: decoded_class[4:0], decoded_alu_op[4:0], reg selects, immediate, shift amount, mem size, flags

### Register File (§4.3)
- **GPRF:** 17×32-bit (R0–R12, MSP, PSP, LR, PC), 2R/1W, combinational read
- **Banked SP:** Handler→MSP, Thread→MSP/PSP based on CONTROL.SPSEL
- **5 SPRs:** APSR (N,Z,C,V flags), IPSR (exception#), EPSR (T-bit=1), PRIMASK (PM bit), CONTROL (SPSEL)
- **EXC_RETURN values:** 0xFFFFFFF1, 0xFFFFFFF9, 0xFFFFFFFD

### NVIC & Exceptions (§5)
- **Arrays:** pending[IRQ_NUM], enable[IRQ_NUM], active[IRQ_NUM], priority[IRQ_NUM] (2-bit default)
- **2-stage synchronizers** for IRQ, NMI, DBGRQ (async inputs)
- **NMI:** edge-detected rising edge after synchronizer
- **Priority arbiter:** lowest number = highest priority
- **Exception entry:** stacking (8 AHB writes) → vector fetch → handler
- **Exception return:** EXC_RETURN detection → unstacking (8 AHB reads)
- **Tail-chaining:** skip unstacking/stacking, vector fetch only (≥6 cycles)
- **Late-arrival:** restart stacking for higher-priority exception

### SysTick (§5.8)
- **24-bit down-counter** (CVR), reload value (RVR), control/status (CSR)
- CSR bits: ENABLE, TICKINT, CLKSOURCE, COUNTFLAG

### Memory & Bus (§6)
- **All FF-based storage**, no SRAM — ~912 bits total
- **AHB-Lite master:** pipelined address/data phases, SINGLE transfers, registered outputs
- **HPROT:** 4'b0011 (data) or 4'b0001 (fetch)
- **No unaligned access** — HardFault if misaligned

### Timing (§7)
- **50 MHz target**, single HCLK domain
- **Pipeline latency:** 3 cycles (ALU), 4 cycles (load), ≥16 cycles (exception entry)
- **Throughput:** 1.0 IPC (sequential ALU), 0.5 IPC (loads/stores)
- **Critical path:** GPRF read → ALU → flags → APSR writeback

### Coding Rules (§8)
- **Reset:** `always_ff @(posedge HCLK or negedge HRESETn)`, async assert, sync deassert
- **All outputs registered** — no combinational output paths
- **Latch prevention** — default assignments in every always_comb branch
- **Tie-off:** unconnected IRQ/NMI/DBGRQ tied low at SoC level

## [mas-orchestration]
# MAS Orchestration Rules

## Workflow Model

Each stage runs in its own workspace. mas_gen is responsible for:
1. Writing the MAS document — the single source of truth
2. Outputting [MAS HANDOFF] blocks to tell the user which workspace to switch to next
3. Reviewing results when other workspaces report back

You do NOT switch context internally. You hand off via explicit [MAS HANDOFF] messages.

## Handoff Protocol

When MAS is complete and ready for the next stage, output:

```
[MAS HANDOFF] → rtl_gen
Module  : <ip_name>
MAS     : <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md
Task    : Implement RTL from MAS §2-§8
Input   : <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md
Output  : <ip_name>/rtl/<ip_name>.sv, <ip_name>/list/<ip_name>.f
Switch  : /workspace rtl_gen → /new-ip-rtl
```

## Quality Gates

| Gate | Condition | Next workspace |
|------|-----------|----------------|
| MAS → RTL | All 9 sections complete | `rtl_gen` → `/new-ip-rtl` |
| RTL → TB | lint: 0 errors, 0 warnings | `tb_gen` → `/new-ip-tb` |
| TB → SIM | TB compiles clean | `sim` → `/compile` |
| SIM → LINT | sim: 0 errors, 0 warnings | `lint` → `/lint-all` |

## File Ownership

| Workspace | Writes | Never modifies |
|-----------|--------|----------------|
| `req_gen` | `<ip>/req/<ip>_requirements.md` | everything else |
| `mas_gen` | `<ip>/mas/<ip>_mas.md` | source files |
| `rtl_gen` | `<ip>/rtl/<ip>.sv`, `<ip>/list/<ip>.f` | `tb_*.sv`, `*.md` |
| `tb_gen`  | `<ip>/tb/tb_<ip>.sv`, `<ip>/tb/tc_<ip>.sv` | `<ip>.sv` |
| `sim`     | `<ip>/sim/sim_report.txt` | source files |
| `lint`    | `<ip>/lint/lint_report.txt` | source files |

## Error Recovery

- RTL lint error → user switches to `rtl_gen`, fixes, re-runs `/lint`
- Sim error (DUT bug) → user switches to `rtl_gen`, fixes RTL
- Sim error (TB bug) → user switches to `tb_gen`, fixes TB
- Max sim iterations → output [MAS BLOCKED] report with details

## [rtl-coding-rules]
# RTL Coding Rules (rtl_gen)

## Mandatory Patterns

```systemverilog
// FF block — always nonblocking
always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) q <= '0;
    else        q <= d;
end

// Comb block — always blocking
always_comb begin
    out = '0;          // default assignment (prevents latch)
    case (sel)
        2'b00: out = a;
        2'b01: out = b;
        default: out = '0;
    endcase
end
```

## Forbidden Patterns

```systemverilog
// NEVER: mixed blocking/nonblocking
always_ff @(posedge clk) begin
    a = b;    // WRONG — blocking in FF block
    c <= d;
end

// NEVER: latch (missing else)
always_comb begin
    if (en) out = data;  // WRONG — no else → latch
end

// NEVER: initial in RTL
initial begin  // WRONG — not synthesizable
    reg_val = 0;
end
```

## Width Rules

- Explicit width on all constants: `8'h00`, `1'b0`, `'0` (all zeros)
- Check width match in assignments — no implicit truncation
- Use `$clog2(N)` for address width calculation

## Reset Convention

- Active-low async reset: `always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)`
- Active-high sync reset: `always_ff @(posedge clk)` with `if (rst)`
- Pick ONE per project — document in spec

# MAS Orchestration Rules

Each stage runs in its own workspace. mas_gen is responsible for:
1. Writing the MAS document — the single source of truth
2. Outputting [MAS HANDOFF] blocks to tell the user which workspace to switch to next
3. Reviewing results when other workspaces report back

## Handoff Protocol

When MAS is complete and ready for the next stage, output:

```
[MAS HANDOFF] → rtl_gen
Module  : <ip_name>
MAS     : <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md
Task    : Implement RTL from MAS §2-§8
Input   : <ip_name>/mas/<ip_name>_mas.md
Output  : <ip_name>/rtl/<ip_name>.sv, <ip_name>/list/<ip_name>.f
Switch  : /workspace rtl_gen → /new-ip-rtl
```

## Quality Gates

| Gate | Condition | Next workspace |
|------|-----------|----------------|
| MAS → RTL | All 9 sections complete | `rtl_gen` → `/new-ip-rtl` |
| RTL → TB | lint: 0 errors, 0 warnings | `tb_gen` → `/new-ip-tb` |
| TB → SIM | TB compiles clean | `sim` → `/compile` |
| SIM → LINT | sim: 0 errors, 0 warnings | `lint` → `/lint-all` |

## File Ownership

| Workspace | Writes | Never modifies |
|-----------|--------|----------------|
| `req_gen` | `<ip>/req/<ip>_requirements.md` | everything else |
| `mas_gen` | `<ip>/mas/<ip>_mas.md` | source files |
| `rtl_gen` | `<ip>/rtl/<ip>.sv`, `<ip>/list/<ip>.f` | `tb_*.sv`, `*.md` |
| `tb_gen`  | `<ip>/tb/tb_<ip>.sv`, `<ip>/tb/tc_<ip>.sv` | `<ip>.sv` |
| `sim`     | `<ip>/sim/sim_report.txt` | source files |
| `lint`    | `<ip>/lint/lint_report.txt` | source files |

- RTL lint error → user switches to `rtl_gen`, fixes, re-runs `/lint`
- Sim error (DUT bug) → user switches to `rtl_gen`, fixes RTL
- Sim error (TB bug) → user switches to `tb_gen`, fixes TB
- Max sim iterations → output [MAS BLOCKED] report with details

## [rtl-coding-rules]
# RTL Coding Rules (rtl_gen)

## Mandatory Patterns

```systemverilog
// FF block — always nonblocking
always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) q <= '0;
    else        q <= d;
end

// Comb block — always blocking
always_comb begin
    out = '0;          // default assignment (prevents latch)
    case (sel)
        2'b00: out = a;
        2'b01: out = b;
        default: out = '0;
    endcase
end
```

## Forbidden Patterns

```systemverilog
// NEVER: mixed blocking/nonblocking
always_ff @(posedge clk) begin
    a = b;    // WRONG — blocking in FF block
    c <= d;
end

// NEVER: latch (missing else)
always_comb begin
    if (en) out = data;  // WRONG — no else → latch
end

// NEVER: initial in RTL
initial begin  // WRONG — not synthesizable
    reg_val = 0;
end
```

## Width Rules

- Explicit width on all constants: `8'h00`, `1'b0`, `'0` (all zeros)
- Check width match in assignments — no implicit truncation
- Use `$clog2(N)` for address width calculation

## Reset Convention

- Active-low async reset: `always_ff @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n)`
- Active-high sync reset: `always_ff @(posedge clk)` with `if (rst)`
- Pick ONE per project — document in spec
